title: Microsoft considers spinning off Xbox and accelerating releases of Fallout, Elder Scrolls and Halo
summary: Reports indicate that the company seeks to accelerate development of its major franchises and does not rule out a total separation of the Xbox division.

title: Nintendo sues Pocketpair over Palworld and experts doubt its chances
summary: A patent expert believes that Nintendo has zero chance of winning the lawsuit against Palworld, but Pocketpair is already paying the price.

title: Wild Terra 2 is offered for free on Steam for a limited time
summary: The medieval open-world MMORPG Wild Terra 2 is available for free on Steam forever if claimed before the offer ends.

title: Shareholders pressure FromSoftware to separate from Bandai Namco
summary: FromSoftware investors are trying to force a break between the company and its publishing partner Bandai Namco.

title: Assassin's Creed creator caught using AI in his new project and his apology generates more controversy
summary: The father of the Assassin's Creed saga was discovered using artificial intelligence in his work, which sparked a controversy that his apology only worsened.

title: Dying Light 2 becomes a platform with the Breach update that opens the game to user-generated content
summary: The Breach update transforms Dying Light 2, allowing players to create and share content similar to a platform.

title: Death Stranding movie will not directly adapt the game's story, it will be an original tale
summary: Death Stranding director confirms that the movie will tell a new story within the game's universe, not a direct adaptation.

title: New details of Silent Hill: Townfall revealed and its setting in a Scottish town
summary: More details have leaked about Silent Hill: Townfall, which takes place in a misty Scottish town.

title: Little Nightmares 3 introduces new character The Puppeteer in the chapter The Backstage
summary: The new Little Nightmares 3 content, The Backstage, introduces the mysterious puppeteer as a new character.

title: Fortnite OG confirms exact date for The Unvaulting event
summary: The Fortnite OG The Unvaulting event now has a release date and time, with an official countdown.

title: Evo 2026 faces problems attracting competitors
summary: The 2026 edition of the Evo tournament records a lower participation from competitors compared to previous years.

title: Sealed Super Mario Bros. reaches $3 million, breaking vintage video game record
summary: A sealed copy of Super Mario Bros. sold for $3 million, setting a new historical record.

title: Firefighters investigate explosion caused by Meta Quest 3 in a Maine home
summary: Authorities investigate a fire in Maine caused by a Meta Quest 3 virtual reality headset.

title: GameStop reward program could lose its best feature, according to a report
summary: A report indicates that GameStop plans to eliminate one of the most valued benefits of its loyalty program.

title: Palworld 1.0 will not delete your data, but they recommend starting over, according to Pocketpair
summary: Pocketpair's head of publishing assures that version 1.0 of Palworld will not erase progress, but suggests restarting for a better experience.

title: Age rating for Oblivion Remastered for Switch 2 appears online
summary: An age rating suggests that The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered could be released on Nintendo Switch 2.

title: Estimated file size of Kingdom Hearts collection for Switch 2 revealed
summary: A leak indicates the space that the Kingdom Hearts collection will take up on Nintendo Switch 2.

title: Resident Evil Code: Veronica was the most popular announcement of Summer Game Fest
summary: According to metrics, the announcement of Resident Evil Code: Veronica generated the most interest during Summer Game Fest.

title: One Piece announces another hiatus and concern grows for Eiichiro Oda, but chapter 1,186 already has a date
summary: The One Piece manga enters a new pause, raising concern for Oda's health, although the next chapter has a confirmed date.

title: Vanillaware wants to bring more games to PC, but it depends on publishers
summary: Vanillaware has expressed its desire to publish more titles on PC, although the decision falls on its publishing partners.
